feedback_loops.label: Feedback Loops & Determinism
feedback_loops.promptlet_template: Does this technology provide fast, reliable feedback? Consider: type safety (compile-time errors), dev mode with hot reload, descriptive error messages AI can parse, deterministic test behavior.
evaluation_questions: Does it have a type system or static analysis?
Are error messages descriptive with file/line numbers?
Is the test environment deterministic?
Does it support dev/watch mode for fast iteration?

documentation_accessibility.label: Documentation Accessibility
documentation_accessibility.promptlet_template: Is documentation machine-readable and accessible to the implementing agent? Consider: structured reference-grade docs vs tutorial-only, text-based vs video, comprehensive coverage of common patterns.
evaluation_questions: Are official docs structured and reference-grade?
Can the agent access and query these docs at implementation time?
Do docs have complete, copy-pasteable code examples?
Are there migration guides for version changes?

decision_space.label: Decision Space & Conventions
decision_space.promptlet_template: Does this technology minimize the number of valid ways to accomplish common tasks? Consider: convention over configuration, linter/formatter ecosystem, opinionated vs flexible.
evaluation_questions: Is there 'one obvious way' for common tasks?
Does linter/formatter tooling exist to enforce consistency?
Are patterns convention-driven or fully flexible?

executability.label: Executability & Runtime Feedback
executability.promptlet_template: Can the AI run this locally and get live feedback? Consider: single-command setup, Docker support, dev mode, structured error logging for runtime debugging.
evaluation_questions: Can it run locally with minimal setup?
Does it support Docker for reproducible environments?
Are runtime errors structured and parseable?
Is there a dev/watch mode for fast iteration?

modularity.label: Scope Isolation & Modularity
modularity.promptlet_template: Does this technology support bounded, isolated changes? Consider: file-based routing, module boundaries, whether small changes require touching many files.
evaluation_questions: Can the AI work on one module without loading the entire codebase?
Does the file structure support focused context?
Are module interfaces clear and documented?

reversibility.label: Reversibility & Cost of Mistakes
reversibility.promptlet_template: Are mistakes cheap to recover from? Consider: migration rollback support, feature flags, idempotent operations, whether state changes are reversible.
evaluation_questions: Does the migration tool support rollback?
Are key operations idempotent (safe to retry)?
Can state changes be reversed without data loss?

safety.label: Security by Default
safety.promptlet_template: Does this technology make the insecure path harder than the secure path? Consider: parameterized queries, built-in CSRF/XSS protection, secure authentication libraries.
evaluation_questions: Are security-critical operations secure by default?
Does the standard API prevent common vulnerabilities?
Is authentication/authorization built-in or manual?

multi_agent.label: Multi-Agent Compatibility
multi_agent.promptlet_template: Can multiple agents work on this codebase in parallel? Consider: file structure that minimizes merge conflicts, auto-generated contracts (OpenAPI, GraphQL), independent testability.
evaluation_questions: Does the file structure minimize merge conflict probability?
Are contracts between components auto-generated and shared?
Can modules be tested independently?

reviewability.label: Reviewability
reviewability.promptlet_template: Can a human reviewer who didn't write the code quickly understand it? Consider: explicit vs implicit behavior, widely understood technology, readable code patterns.
evaluation_questions: Is the technology widely understood for effective review?
Does the code show explicit behavior (vs. metaprogramming/magic)?
Can reviewers assess correctness without deep framework knowledge?

ai_learning_curve.label: AI Learning Curve
ai_learning_curve.promptlet_template: How many iterations does the AI typically need to produce correct output? Consider: common patterns AI gets wrong, subtle gotchas, training data quality for this technology.
evaluation_questions: Are there patterns AI commonly gets wrong with this technology?
Does the AI produce correct code on the first attempt for common tasks?
Are there subtle gotchas that require deep understanding?
